contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 75
- Description: tribomirimbahia.truesight.me integrated into truesight.me as a Fundraisers program — full 4-PR arc
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/tokenomics/pull/277
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context/pull/116
  
  Details:
  Closes Gary's question 'how do we integrate https://tribomirimbahia.truesight.me/ into https://truesight.me/?'. After exploring options the architecture landed on: a generic Fundraisers program page on truesight.me that filters the canonical managed-ledger registry by program=fundraiser. Adding a future fundraiser becomes a 3-step drop-in (Sheet row + publisher run + explorer subdomain); the truesight.me page picks it up automatically.
  
  Round 1 — Sheets API direct write:
  - Added column AC 'Program' to Shipment Ledger Listing on the Main Ledger spreadsheet (1GE7PUq...). Backfilled all 17 active rows by reading their Transaction Type (col H) and mapping to one of three program-family rollups: agroverse (Defi Pre-Purchase + DAO financed → 14 rows AGL0..AGL15), sunmint (Merchant Green Pledge → 2 rows SEF1, PP1), fundraiser (Donation → 1 row TBM = Tribo Bahia Mirim). Used tokenomics-schema@get-data-io... SA which can bypass the listing's column protection.
  
  Round 2 — tokenomics PR #277 (merged):
  - snapshot_managed_ledgers.py threads col AC through to per-ledger JSON as a top-level 'program' field (TBM.json now has program: 'fundraiser', SEF1.json has program: 'sunmint', etc.).
  - New write_index() emits treasury-cache/managed-ledgers/_index.json containing one entry per active ledger (ledger_id, program, status, description, ledger_url, snapshot_url, summary). One fetch lets a consumer like truesight.me/fundraisers.html filter program=fundraiser without N round-trips. Skipped on --ledger single-runs to avoid shrinking the index to one entry.
  - Ran the publisher locally; pushed to treasury-cache/main. 12 active per-ledger JSONs now carry program; _index.json live at https://raw.githubusercontent.com/TrueSightDAO/treasury-cache/main/managed-ledgers/_index.json with 9 agroverse + 2 sunmint + 1 fundraiser (the 5 COMPLETED/SUSPENDED rows are skipped by the publisher).
  
  Round 3 — agentic_ai_context PR #116 (merged) + personal memory:
  - MANAGED_LEDGER_EXPLORER_PATTERN.md 'Register in Shipment Ledger Listing' rewritten to expand the column table with each col by letter (A B C D H L AB AC) so AC is visible alongside the other required fields. Marked AC as ⚠️ REQUIRED with the three allowed values + the natural Transaction Type → Program mapping. Added a note that AI sessions MUST prompt the operator when AC isn't obvious. Updated SA recommendation to tokenomics-schema (works on the listing's protection).
  - Added ~/.claude/projects/-Users-garyjob-Applications/memory/reference_managed_ledger_program_column.md + MEMORY.md index entry so the col AC requirement surfaces in every future Claude session loaded against this workspace.
  
  Round 4 — truesight_me_beta PRs #61 + #62 (both merged):
  - #61: new fundraisers.html mirroring sunmint.html structure (shared site header, hero, sections, footer). Fetches _index.json on page load, filters program=fundraiser, renders fundraiser cards. 'How a fundraiser works' section explains the donation → currency conversion → Pix disbursement chain (the same chain that's now end-to-end automated via dapp/currency_conversion.html + currency_conversion_processing.gs). Added 'Fundraisers' to the Projects dropdown on index.html so it's discoverable.
  - Card renderer has a small EXTRA hash keyed on ledger_id for hand-curated metadata the JSON index doesn't carry (program_name, explorer_url, site_url, image). E.g. TBM gets the 'Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira' full name + the capoeira.agroverse.shop practice-platform link. New fundraisers with just an _index.json entry render with sensible defaults (title=ledger_id, explorer_url=lowercased subdomain on truesight.me); add an EXTRA entry to enrich.
  - #62 (follow-up after Gary's local review): two layout corrections.
    (a) Card was rendering as a single huge tile because .shipments-cards is the mobile-only fallback class — the standard sunmint/agroverse pattern is .shipment-table for desktop (>=641px) + .shipments-cards for mobile (<=640px), both populated from the same JS. Restructured fundraisers.html the same way: <table class='shipment-table'> for desktop with Image | Fundraiser | Description | Details columns, .shipments-cards for mobile, JS extracted into a shared fundraiserView(l) builder so both renderers see byte-identical data.
    (b) Within the Details cell, 'View Ledger' + 'Practice platform' buttons were inline-block with just a space between them, wrapping awkwardly. Now stacked vertically in a flex column with var(--space-xs) gap, left-aligned.
  
  Net result: visiting https://truesight.me/ → Projects dropdown → Fundraisers → see a table with TBM + 'View Ledger' (→ tribomirimbahia.truesight.me explorer) + 'Practice platform' (→ capoeira.agroverse.shop). End-to-end flow finally has a discovery path on the main DAO site.
  
  Adding a new fundraiser is now a 3-step drop-in:
  1. Add row to Shipment Ledger Listing with col AC = 'fundraiser'
  2. Run snapshot_managed_ledgers.py (refreshes per-ledger JSON + _index.json)
  3. Spin up the explorer subdomain
  The fundraisers page picks it up on next load. Optionally add an EXTRA hash entry in fundraisers.html to enrich the card with program_name, explorer_url, site_url, image.
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 125.00
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
